code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a phrase for each predicate
    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        """
        Convert a single triple into a clause that explicitly starts with its subject.
        """
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Patterns that describe the relationship after the subject
        patterns = {
            "author": f"was written by {o}",
            "isbnNumber": f"has ISBN {o}",
            "followedBy": f"is followed by {o}",
            "precededBy": f"is preceded by {o}",
            "language": f"is written in {o}",
            "mediaType": f"is available as {o}",
            "numberOfPages": f"has {o} pages",
            "country": f"is published in {o}",
            "almaMater": f"studied at {o}",
            "doctoralAdvisor": f"was advised by {o}",
            "nationality": f"is {o}",
            "residence": f"lives in {o}",
            "codenCode": f"has CODEN {o}",
            "issnNumber": f"has ISSN {o}",
            "LCCN_number": f"has LCCN {o}",
            "abbreviation": f"is abbreviated as {o}",
            "academicDiscipline": f"belongs to the discipline of {o}",
            "publisher": f"is published by {o}",
            "firstPublicationYear": f"was first published in {o}",
            "editor": f"is edited by {o}",
            "impactFactor": f"has an impact factor of {o}",
            "oclcNumber": f"has OCLC number {o}",
            "libraryofCongressClassification": f"is classified under {o}",
            "genre": f"is a {o} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"is a {o} work",
            "eissnNumber": f"has e‑ISSN {o}",
            "frequency": f"is issued {o}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered in {o}",
            "leader": f"has leader {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"was born in {o}",
            "affiliation": f"is affiliated with {o}",
            "city": f"is located in {o}",
            "president": f"has president {o}",
            "state": f"is in the state of {o}",
            "spokenIn": f"is spoken in {o}",
            "influencedBy": f"is influenced by {o}",
            "family": f"belongs to the {o} family",
            "ethnicGroup": f"is associated with the {o} ethnic group",
            "largestCity": f"has largest city {o}",
            "location": f"is located in {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {o}",
            "notableWork": f"has notable work {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"is owned by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "founder": f"was founded by {o}",
            "capital": f"is capital {o}",
            "demonym": f"has demonym {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"has leader title {o}",
            "nickname": f"is nicknamed {o}",
            "genus": f"belongs to the genus {o}",
            "releaseDate": f"was released on {o}",
            "birthDate": f"was born on {o}",
        }
        clause = patterns.get(p, f"{p} {o}")
        # Ensure the clause starts with the subject
        return f"{s} {clause}"

    # Determine the primary subject (the subject of the first triple)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build phrases for the main subject first, then for any secondary subjects
    main_phrases = [phrase(t, True) for t in triples if t.subject == main_subject]
    other_phrases = [phrase(t, False) for t in triples if t.subject != main_subject]

    all_phrases = main_phrases + other_phrases

    # Combine the phrases into a fluent sentence
    if len(all_phrases) == 1:
        sentence = all_phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + all_phrases[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize the first character of the final sentence
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

metrics.error: predict.<locals>.phrase() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
